THOUGHT:
Reviewing the current citations, I have covered summarizing research (Weng 2024), datasets/benchmarks and comparing findings (Koh 2020), and highlighting research gaps. However, I still lack citations for specific conceptual failure modes (Category 2) and supporting arguments about why models fail in deployment (Category 7). A highly relevant and foundational paper for this is "Shortcut Learning in Deep Neural Networks" by Geirhos et al. (2020), which extensively documents how models exploit dataset biases and spurious correlations instead of learning robust, causal features. This will be crucial for explaining real-world brittleness, supporting our failure analysis, and motivating the need for better evaluation methodologies.
